title: armeria plantaginea subsp. castellana
intro: Armeria plantaginea subsp. castellana, commonly known as the Castilian sea thrift, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to provide health benefits such as wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. Armeria plantaginea subsp. castellana is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
